sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.60 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Suppose that$H$is a non-trivial normal subgroup of a finite group~$G$such that the factor-group$G/H$is isomorphic to one of the simple groups$L_n(q)$,$n 3$. Is it true that$G$has an element whose order is distinct from the order of any element in$G/H$? { V.\,D.\,Mazurov} Yes, it is true: for$n 4$proved in (A.\,V.\,Zavarnitsine, Siberian Math.~J., 49 (2008), 246--256), and for$n=4$in (M.\,A.\,Grech...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
